assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and Node.js 18 or later.
The developer has a local application project directory ready to deploy.
No Vercel account, project link, or deployment exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Vercel account and deploy a local project so the CLI returns a deployment URL.

first_success.outcome: The local project is built and deployed to Vercel and the CLI writes a deployment URL to standard output.
first_success.observable_signal: The vercel command finishes successfully and prints the Deployment URL.

route_selection.rule: Getting started states every Vercel workflow starts with the CLI; Dashboard Git deploy remains a documented alternative whose account and Git gates still need equal-route judgment for signup identity.

candidates.name: CLI install, login, and vercel deploy
candidates.reason: Vendor getting-started core path, but signup identity options and vercel login prompts are not fully enumerated for a verified field count.

candidates.name: Dashboard New Project Git deploy
candidates.reason: Presented as deploy without the CLI; requires Git provider connection and is a consequential alternate surface after account creation.

required_path.action: Create a Vercel account from the public sign-up page.

required_fields.label: Sign-up identity provider choice
required_fields.notes: Public signup shows Terms agreement and Show other options; exact required identity buttons were not fully enumerated in the fetched UI.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy agreement

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a Vercel account that can authenticate for CLI login.

required_fields.label: Package manager install command for vercel

required_path.observable_result: The vercel command is available locally.

required_path.action: Run vercel login and complete the authentication prompts.

required_fields.label: CLI login method and prompts
required_fields.notes: Docs say follow the prompts; email, GitHub, and other methods exist in CLI reference without a single observed required field set.

required_path.observable_result: The CLI session is authenticated to the Vercel account.

required_path.action: From the project directory, run vercel and complete any first-time project linking prompts.

required_fields.label: First-time project linking prompts
required_fields.notes: Linking docs describe interactive team and project prompts; exact required prompts for a brand-new account were not freshly observed.

required_path.observable_result: Vercel builds and deploys the project and prints a deployment URL.

external_gates.description: Identity-provider authorization if signup or login uses GitHub or another external IdP.

unavoidable_waits.description: Vercel must build the project before the deployment URL is available.

platform_outcomes.description: The vercel command detects the framework, builds the project, and writes the Deployment URL to stdout.

excluded.item: Vercel Plugin or Agent Skills installation
excluded.reason: Getting started presents these after the core CLI deploy sequence for AI coding agents.
Optional extensions beyond the first getting-started deploy.
Documentation is evidence, not a counted action.

sources.title: Vercel Sign Up
evidence_supported: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy agreement

uncertainties.question: Which signup identity path is required or shortest on vercel.com/signup, and which vercel login method is the default for a new account?
uncertainties.impact: Required fields and external IdP gates for account creation and CLI auth remain unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots of signup options and a fresh-account vercel login transcript.

uncertainties.question: For a brand-new account, which interactive vercel project-linking prompts are mandatory before the first deployment URL appears?
uncertainties.impact: Field and action counts for a verified CLI path cannot be finalized.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh-account CLI walkthrough from an empty Vercel team through first vercel deploy.
